item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know that common consensus is that this guy is really underdeveloped and undercooked, but I have a soft spot for him.

+1 for Stage Face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to see an interview with this guy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really enjoyed this, and the ending. It was kinda cliche but I enjoyed it for what it was and the images it conveyed. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Factoring in the edits, I am calling this an improvement. A pity it took me THIS long to realise that edits have been made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to know more about its backstory. I think the SCP would be much more effective if there was more insight into its origins, purpose, identity, etc. Some interview logs would be nice.

item.title: Admin Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
As we do not allow one user to have multiple accounts on the site, the DownsenBranches account will be blocked from the mainsite.

If you would like to discuss this action, please contact me or another site administrator.

Do not reply if you are not a staff member. This is a closed staff post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh gosh they really did it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oop sorry. Posted from my backup account.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your feedback. It actually confirms some of the doubts I was having myself

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last paragraph seems tacked on and cheesy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It generally makes sense but also seems like an unnecessary risk. Maybe the reason is just that the performances are interesting enough to observe and transcribe. That might have the pros outweigh the cons.

Many real theaters discourage filming and photography, so maybe that's why the audience has to be live?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like some of the suggested imagery, but it overall felt very vague and under-developed. I think making this less overtly human and more inhabiting a human shell might give you more opportunities to develop this. There's the seed of a good idea here but this form just isn't selling it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't you think it would be preferable to have an SCP who is willing to cooperate with the Foundation rather than one that is uncooperative. In my opinion, if the Foundation had a way to make an SCP more cooperative with their research and questions why wouldn't they take that opportunity?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps it is serviceable, but the anomaly is rather dull. The ending you're going for does not really resonate strongly. Sure, it can force people who disrespect his music to be his musician postmortem forever. But not everyone knows music or is good at it or have an idea as to what it means when the conductor does a certain action.

Won't this symphony descend to a cacophony eventually? And that might be the ending that is more desirable than what we're stuck with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why is SCP-3073's cooperation needed? Why does it need its audience physically present? Is watching with video cameras more dangerous?

From what I gather, it can only take people who watch its performances, so letting it have no audience seems like the safest course of action.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You bring up some good points. I never really considered the hair cutting possibility. Also maybe I should make it more clear that it doesn't require an audience but having one makes it more cooperative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How do the people get little? Can they be removed from the stage? Why hasn't anyone tried to cut its hair? Why does it need an audience in the first place? How was it obtained?

There's too many questions and I feel like you'd be better off adding details than removing them. It's just that becoming a cast member in itself isn't scary. Aesthetically, it's got an interesting look, but that's all it has going for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would removing the "replacement" aspect help that in any way or no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to echo the above sentiment by saying this: It's not terribly written, but ultimately, it's predictable. It doesn't quite feel intriguing enough to stand with the other recent articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually didn't know that. Thanks for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, not feeling it. When I got to "conductor with a miniature theatrical stage for a face" I guessed what the rest of the SCP would be right away. I don't generally like SCPs unless they're unexpected in some respect. Also, "takes another person to replace missing members" is actually a rather common trait for SCPs, in my experience, and doesn't really add anything to the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a decent SCP. Short, simple, and not too boring. There are just a few minor changes I would like to recommend. First of all, in the containment procedures, you said "T"he room is to be equipped with lights capable of being dimmed by a simple, handheld remote, the possession of which will be given to SCP-3073 for it to use at its own discretion." This is a bit awkward to read, and may be better if you removed "the possession of" from it. Second, you referred to "theater edict." I believe what you meant to say was "theater etiquette".

Third, you indicated that it is fluent in all known languages. You may wish to shorten the list of languages it can speak to a list of its preferred ones, perhaps stating that it is fluent in English, French and German, as a humanoid that can speak all languages seems a bit overpowered.

Otherwise, this is decent. It's somewhat interesting, and it's short and simple enough that the concept doesn't get boring. +1
